domain: Tuareg Imzad (women's single-string bowed gourd-fiddle from the Sahara — Algeria/Mali/Niger; UNESCO Intangible Heritage; one horsehair string on calabash resonator with cross-shaped two-part bridge; melodic range entirely from fingerboard pressure + harmonic-series overtones; no frets — continuous pitch)
specific_mechanism: IMZAD-MONOSTRING: single-channel rich-spectrum allocation for LLM compute. (1) Force a single low-cost channel C — one attention head, one MoE expert, or one embedding axis — to carry K-tone rich information via explicit harmonic-basis projection. (2) Output y_C = Σ_k α_k · cos(kωτ_C + φ_k) where ω is a learned per-channel fundamental and α_k are sparse top-K coefficients. (3) Continuous-pitch fingering — ω varies continuously per token, not quantized into discrete bins. (4) Spectral compression: a single such channel substitutes for K parallel discrete-pitch channels at far lower parameter cost.
llm_application: IMZAD-MONOSTRING: single-channel harmonic-basis spectral allocation in transformer attention/MoE. Differs from SpecQuant 2511.11663 (Fourier-domain quantization for ultra-low-bit LLM compression — global per-tensor not single-channel continuous-pitch), Harmonizer 2025 (multimodal heterogeneous-signal tokenization — not single-channel overtone), Graph-Spectral Decomposition 2504.19583 (graph spectral for parameter coordination — not single-channel harmonic), Spectral Modulation EMNLP 2024 (modulation on weight matrices — not single-channel harmonic-basis projection). Differs from R402 TUVAN-IGIL (multi-string overtone), R416 XALAM-FREQ (discrete xalam strings), R431 TAONGA-MICRO-BAND (micro-band quantization), R440 TUVAN-PHANTOM-CHANNEL (4-head phantom 5th-channel emergent), R279 PTCH (within-head SVD integer-ratio constraint — different invariant, weight not output) by EXPLICIT SINGLE-CHANNEL CONTINUOUS-FREQUENCY HARMONIC-BASIS PROJECTION + SPARSE TOP-K COEFFICIENT.
novelty_claim: SpecQuant + Harmonizer + Graph-Spectral + Spectral-Modulation cover spectral LLM compression + modulation paradigm. IMZAD-MONOSTRING adds (a) single-channel continuous-frequency fundamental + (b) sparse top-K harmonic-coefficient selection + (c) channel-level not tensor-level spectral allocation. Recombination — Fourier basis is mainstream.
